exercises.name: Box Breathing
exercises.description: A calming technique used by Navy SEALs. Breathe in a box pattern to reduce stress and anxiety.

exercises.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
exercises.description: Use your five senses to ground yourself in the present moment and reduce anxiety.

steps.detail: Look around you and notice five things you can see right now.
Reach out and notice four things you can physically feel.
Listen carefully and identify three sounds around you.
Notice two scents in your environment.
Focus on one taste — even if it's just the inside of your mouth.

exercises.name: Journaling Prompts
exercises.description: Thoughtful prompts inspired by CBT to help you process emotions through writing.
prompts: What would you tell a close friend going through the same thing?
What is one small thing that brought you comfort recently?
Describe a time you overcame something you thought you couldn't handle.
What emotions are you feeling right now? Can you name at least three?
What is something you're grateful for today, no matter how small?
If your anxiety could talk, what would it say? How would you respond?
Write about a place where you feel completely safe and at peace.
What is one thing you can do today to take care of yourself?
Describe your perfect day. What does it look like?
What boundaries do you need to set to protect your well-being?
Write a letter to your future self. What do you want them to know?
What are three strengths you have that help you through tough times?

exercises.name: Positive Affirmations
exercises.description: Gentle reminders of your worth and strength. Read them slowly and let them sink in.
affirmations: I am worthy of love and kindness, especially from myself.
It's okay to take things one moment at a time.
My feelings are valid, even when they're hard.
I have overcome difficult things before, and I can do it again.
I don't have to be perfect to be enough.
It's okay to ask for help. That takes courage.
I am more resilient than I give myself credit for.
This feeling is temporary. It will pass.
I am doing the best I can, and that is enough.
My struggles do not define me.
